content:
Key Findings

  • Preston City Council spent £205.9M across 46,711 transactions between 2019/20 and 2025/26
  • All £205.9M of spending was categorised as 'Unknown' payment type
  • The council's spending data shows no breakdown of payment methods or types
  • This lack of transparency makes it difficult to analyse how the council processes payments

Understanding Preston's Spending Data

Preston City Council published spending data for seven financial years, totalling £205.9M across 46,711 transactions. This data provides valuable insights into how public money is spent. However, the lack of payment type information presents challenges for detailed analysis.

The £205.9M figure represents all transactions recorded by the council during this period. Despite the substantial amount, the data does not differentiate between payment methods such as bank transfers, cheques, or electronic payments. This absence of detail limits the ability to assess efficiency or identify potential areas for improvement.

The Importance of Payment Type Transparency

Understanding payment types is crucial for several reasons. It helps identify potential cost savings, such as reducing fees associated with specific payment methods. For instance, electronic payments are generally more cost-effective than cheques. Additionally, tracking payment types can reveal patterns in supplier relationships and payment processing efficiency.

Unfortunately, Preston City Council's data does not provide this level of detail. The £205.9M spent is categorised as 'Unknown', which means the council has not specified the payment method for any of these transactions. This lack of transparency makes it difficult to analyse how the council manages its finances effectively.

Comparing with Other Councils

While Preston City Council's data lacks payment type information, other councils often provide more detailed breakdowns. For example, some councils may categorise payments as 'Bank Transfer', 'Cheque', or 'Electronic Payment'. This level of detail allows for better benchmarking and comparison.

Without this information, it is challenging to assess whether Preston City Council is optimising its payment processes. The £205.9M spent could potentially be managed more efficiently with clearer payment type data. This highlights the importance of transparency in public spending.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Preston City Council asking for a breakdown of payment types used for transactions
  • Attend council committee meetings to discuss the importance of payment type transparency and how it can improve financial management
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see if payment methods are specified in supplier agreements
  • Write to your local councillor expressing your concern about the lack of payment type information in the council's spending data
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and identify other areas for potential transparency improvements
This article is based on Preston City Council's published spending data covering 2019/20 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
